heading: 9.7.14.5.Matrix multiply-accumulate operation usingmmainstruction (part 12)
content:
```
// Store a single 8x8 matrix using 64-bit addressing
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x1.shared.b16 [addr], {r};

// Store two 8x8 matrices in column-major format
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<2>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x2.trans.shared::cta.b16 [addr], {r0, r1};

// Store four 8x8 matrices
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<4>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.x4.b16 [addr], {r0, r1, r2, r3};

// Store a single 16x8 matrix using generic addressing
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n8.x1.trans.shared.b8 [addr], {r};

// Store two 16x8 matrices
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<2>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n8.x2.trans.shared::cta.b8 [addr],{r0, r1};

// Store four 16x8 matrices
.reg .b64 addr;
.reg .b32 r<4>;
stmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n8.x4.b8 [addr], {r0, r1, r2, r3};
```

##### 9.7.14.5.17. [Warp-level matrix transpose instruction: `movmatrix`](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-movmatrix)

`movmatrix`

Transpose a matrix in registers across the warp.

Syntax

```
movmatrix.sync.aligned.shape.trans.type d, a;

.shape  = {.m8n8};
.type   = {.b16};
```

Description

Move a row-major matrix across all threads in a warp, reading elements from source `a`, and
writing the transposed elements to destination `d`.

The `.shape` qualifier indicates the dimensions of the matrix being transposed. Each matrix
element holds 16-bit data as indicated by the `.type` qualifier.

The mandatory `.sync` qualifier indicates that `movmatrix` causes the executing thread to wait
until all threads in the warp execute the same `movmatrix` instruction before resuming execution.

The mandatory `.aligned` qualifier indicates that all threads in the warp must execute the same
`movmatrix` instruction. In conditionally executed code, a `movmatrix` instruction should only
be used if it is known that all threads in the warp evaluate the condition identically, otherwise
the behavior is undefined.

Operands `a` and `d` are 32-bit registers containing fragments of the input matrix and the
resulting matrix respectively. The mandatory qualifier `.trans` indicates that the resulting
matrix in `d` is a transpose of the input matrix specified by `a`.

Each thread in a warp holds a fragment of a row of the input matrix, with thread 0 holding the first
fragment in register `a`, and so on. A group of four threads holds an entire row of the input
matrix as shown in [Figure 109](#mma-movmatrix-fragments-src).





[Figure: mma-movmatrix-fragments-src]
MOVMATRIX SOURCE MATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT

Title: "movmatrix: matrix fragment in source operand a"

OVERVIEW:
This figure describes the source operand "a" fragment layout for the movmatrix instruction. The matrix is laid out across 32 lanes (laneid 0 through 31) in a warp, organized into 8 rows and 8 columns (col0 through col7).

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows: row0 through row7 (8 rows total)
- Columns: col0 through col7 (8 columns total)
- Each cell contains a laneid assignment and src=a designation
- Each pair of adjacent columns shares a single cell entry (col0+col1 share one cell, col2+col3 share one cell, col4+col5 share one cell, col6+col7 share one cell)

DETAILED CELL-BY-CELL LAYOUT:

row0:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 0,  src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 1,  src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 2,  src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 3,  src=a

row1:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 4,  src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 5,  src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 6,  src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 7,  src=a

row2:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 8,  src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 9,  src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 10, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 11, src=a

row3:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 12, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 13, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 14, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 15, src=a

row4:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 16, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 17, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 18, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 19, src=a

row5:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 20, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 21, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 22, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 23, src=a

row6:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 24, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 25, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 26, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 27, src=a

row7:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 28, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 29, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 30, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 31, src=a

GROUPING PATTERN:
- Each lane (laneid 0-31) occupies one merged cell spanning two adjacent columns within a single row.
- Lanes 0-3 occupy row0 (covering all 8 columns in groups of 2).
- Lanes 4-7 occupy row1.
- Lanes 8-11 occupy row2.
- Lanes 12-15 occupy row3.
- Lanes 16-19 occupy row4.
- Lanes 20-23 occupy row5.
- Lanes 24-27 occupy row6.
- Lanes 28-31 occupy row7.
- Within each row, 4 lanes are assigned sequentially across the 4 column-pair groups.
- Each lane covers 2 columns worth of data (col pair), indicating each lane holds 2 elements packed into its register(s) for this fragment.

LANE-TO-COLUMN MAPPING PATTERN:
- Within each row, lane N*4+0 covers col0-col1, lane N*4+1 covers col2-col3, lane N*4+2 covers col4-col5, lane N*4+3 covers col6-col7 (where N = row index 0-7).

SOURCE DESIGNATION:
- All cells are labeled src=a, confirming this is the source operand "a" fragment.

DATA TYPE:
- No explicit data type (.f16, .bf16, .tf32, .f32, etc.) is labeled in this figure. The data type is not specified within the figure itself.

MATRIX DIMENSIONS:
- The layout shows 8 rows x 8 columns = 64 element positions total, distributed across 32 lanes (each lane holding 2 column positions per row). This corresponds to the source matrix "a" for the movmatrix instruction. Explicit M x N x K dimensions are not labeled in this figure.

MOVMATRIX SOURCE MATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT

Title: "movmatrix: matrix fragment in source operand a"

OVERVIEW:
This figure describes the source operand "a" fragment layout for the movmatrix instruction. The matrix is laid out across 32 lanes (laneid 0 through 31) in a warp, organized into 8 rows and 8 columns (col0 through col7).

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows: row0 through row7 (8 rows total)
- Columns: col0 through col7 (8 columns total)
- Each cell contains a laneid assignment and src=a designation
- Each pair of adjacent columns shares a single cell entry (col0+col1 share one cell, col2+col3 share one cell, col4+col5 share one cell, col6+col7 share one cell)

DETAILED CELL-BY-CELL LAYOUT:

row0:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 0,  src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 1,  src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 2,  src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 3,  src=a

  col2-col3: %laneid = 5,  src=a

  col2-col3: %laneid = 9,  src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 10, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 11, src=a

row3:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 12, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 13, src=a

row4:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 16, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 17, src=a

  col6-col7: %laneid = 19, src=a

row5:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 20, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 21, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 22, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 23, src=a

row6:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 24, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 25, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 26, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 27, src=a

row7:
  col0-col1: %laneid = 28, src=a
  col2-col3: %laneid = 29, src=a
  col4-col5: %laneid = 30, src=a
  col6-col7: %laneid = 31, src=a

GROUPING PATTERN:
- Each lane (laneid 0-31) occupies one merged cell spanning two adjacent columns within a single row.
- Lanes 0-3 occupy row0 (covering all 8 columns in groups of 2).
- Lanes 4-7 occupy row1.
- Lanes 8-11 occupy row2.
- Lanes 12-15 occupy row3.
- Lanes 16-19 occupy row4.
- Lanes 20-23 occupy row5.
- Lanes 24-27 occupy row6.
- Lanes 28-31 occupy row7.
- Within each row, 4 lanes are assigned sequentially across the 4 column-pair groups.
- Each lane covers 2 columns worth of data (col pair), indicating each lane holds 2 elements packed into its register(s) for this fragment.

LANE-TO-COLUMN MAPPING PATTERN:
- Within each row, lane N*4+0 covers col0-col1, lane N*4+1 covers col2-col3, lane N*4+2 covers col4-col5, lane N*4+3 covers col6-col7 (where N = row index 0-7).

SOURCE DESIGNATION:
- All cells are labeled src=a, confirming this is the source operand "a" fragment.

DATA TYPE:
- No explicit data type (.f16, .bf16, .tf32, .f32, etc.) is labeled in this figure. The data type is not specified within the figure itself.

MATRIX DIMENSIONS:
- The layout shows 8 rows x 8 columns = 64 element positions total, distributed across 32 lanes (each lane holding 2 column positions per row). This corresponds to the source matrix "a" for the movmatrix instruction. Explicit M x N x K dimensions are not labeled in this figure.


Figure 109 movmatrix source matrix fragment layout

Each thread in a warp holds a fragment of a column of the result matrix, with thread 0 holding the
first fragment in register `d`, and so on. A group of four threads holds an entire column of the
result matrix as shown in [Figure 110](#mma-movmatrix-fragments-dst).





[Figure: mma-movmatrix-fragments-dst]
MOVMATRIX RESULT MATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT - DESTINATION OPERAND D

OVERVIEW:
This figure describes the fragment layout for the movmatrix instruction's destination operand d. The matrix is an 8x8 layout (8 columns x 8 rows), distributed across 32 lanes (laneid 0 through 31). Each lane holds exactly one element, stored in register dst=d.

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (left axis): col0 through col7 (8 column entries, representing matrix columns)
- Columns (top axis): row0 through row7 (8 row entries, representing matrix rows)
- Each cell contains: %laneid = N and dst=d

NOTE ON AXIS NAMING: The figure labels the horizontal axis as row0-row7 and the vertical axis as col0-col7. Each cell spans two row-columns (pairs of row columns are merged per cell).

DETAILED CELL MAPPING (col x row -> laneid, register):

col0:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 0,  dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 1,  dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 2,  dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 3,  dst=d

col1:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 4,  dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 5,  dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 6,  dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 7,  dst=d

col2:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 8,  dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 9,  dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 10, dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 11, dst=d

col3:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 12, dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 13, dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 14, dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 15, dst=d

col4:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 16, dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 17, dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 18, dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 19, dst=d

col5:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 20, dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 21, dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 22, dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 23, dst=d

col6:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 24, dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 25, dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 26, dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 27, dst=d

col7:
  row0, row1: %laneid = 28, dst=d
  row2, row3: %laneid = 29, dst=d
  row4, row5: %laneid = 30, dst=d
  row6, row7: %laneid = 31, dst=d

LANE-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete list, laneid -> matrix position):
laneid  0: col0, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid  1: col0, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid  2: col0, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid  3: col0, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid  4: col1, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid  5: col1, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid  6: col1, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid  7: col1, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid  8: col2, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid  9: col2, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 10: col2, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 11: col2, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 12: col3, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid 13: col3, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 14: col3, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 15: col3, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 16: col4, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid 17: col4, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 18: col4, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 19: col4, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 20: col5, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid 21: col5, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 22: col5, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 23: col5, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 24: col6, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid 25: col6, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 26: col6, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 27: col6, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 28: col7, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid 29: col7, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 30: col7, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 31: col7, row6-row7, dst=d

GROUPING PATTERN:
- Each column (col0-col7) is handled by 4 consecutive lanes.
- col0: lanes 0-3; col1: lanes 4-7; col2: lanes 8-11; col3: lanes 12-15
- col4: lanes 16-19; col5: lanes 20-23; col6: lanes 24-27; col7: lanes 28-31
- Within each column group of 4 lanes, each lane covers a pair of rows (row0-1, row2-3, row4-5, row6-7).
- Each lane holds one element in register dst=d (single register, no packing noted explicitly in figure).

DATA TYPE: Not explicitly specified in this figure. The register is labeled dst=d with no type annotation shown.

REGISTER PACKING: Each lane holds one value in a single register named d (dst=d). No multi-element packing is indicated in this figure.

OVERVIEW:
This figure describes the fragment layout for the movmatrix instruction's destination operand d. The matrix is an 8x8 layout (8 columns x 8 rows), distributed across 32 lanes (laneid 0 through 31). Each lane holds exactly one element, stored in register dst=d.

- Rows (left axis): col0 through col7 (8 column entries, representing matrix columns)

laneid  9: col2, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 10: col2, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 11: col2, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 12: col3, row0-row1, dst=d
laneid 13: col3, row2-row3, dst=d
laneid 14: col3, row4-row5, dst=d
laneid 15: col3, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 16: col4, row0-row1, dst=d

laneid 19: col4, row6-row7, dst=d
laneid 20: col5, row0-row1, dst=d

- Each lane holds one element in register dst=d (single register, no packing noted explicitly in figure).

DATA TYPE: Not explicitly specified in this figure. The register is labeled dst=d with no type annotation shown.

REGISTER PACKING: Each lane holds one value in a single register named d (dst=d). No multi-element packing is indicated in this figure.


Figure 110 movmatrix result matrix fragment layout

PTX ISA Notes

Introduced in PTX ISA version 7.8.

Target ISA Notes

Requires `sm_75` or higher.

Examples

```
.reg .b32 d, a;
movmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.trans.b16 d, a;
```
